Customize Windows Terminal with oh-my-posh (PowerShell Prompt + Windows Terminal Setup)
If you spend most of your day in a terminal, staring at the same plain prompt gets boring fast. This post walks through making Windows PowerShell pretty.

This was written in 2019. The oh-my-posh setup commands here are for v2. oh-my-posh has since moved to v3+ which is cross-platform but uses different config commands. Check the oh-my-posh official docs for the current installation instructions if you're setting up fresh.
One of the customizations below requires version 1903 specifically.
I also use PowerShell, so this post focuses on PowerShell customization.
Default PowerShell prompt looks like this:
We're going to upgrade it to this:
language-ps shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast
First time installing modules, NuGet will ask a bunch of questions — just
through them. (I'm not screenshotting since I'm already past this.)
in PowerShell to see your PowerShell profile path:
language-cmd shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

Import-Module posh-git
Import-Module oh-my-posh
Set-Theme Agnoster
Now every time PowerShell starts, the prompt is themed automatically.
. The official GitHub README has the
Now my favorite part — Windows Terminal. Microsoft released this in 2019 as a modern replacement for the old console host:
(Optional) Combined with the oh-my-posh setup above
Install via the Microsoft Store — click
After install, search "Windows Terminal" — I'd recommend pinning it to the start menu:
On launch it'll look similar to plain PowerShell. Click the down arrow next to the tabs → Settings:
— you'll see three objects, each representing a shell:
The order matches the dropdown order. For PowerShell, edit the first one:
Annotated example of what I edited:
Background image path can't contain non-ASCII characters
. Without a nerd font / powerline-aware font, oh-my-posh's special characters render incorrectly.

html .light .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-light);background: var(--shiki-light-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-light-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-light-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-light-text-decoration);}html.light .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-light);background: var(--shiki-light-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-light-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-light-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-light-text-decoration);}html .default .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-default);background: var(--shiki-default-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-default-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-default-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-default-text-decoration);}html .dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}html.dark .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-dark);background: var(--shiki-dark-bg);font-style: var(--shiki-dark-font-style);font-weight: var(--shiki-dark-font-weight);text-decoration: var(--shiki-dark-text-decoration);}

Staring at the same plain PowerShell prompt all day gets old. This post walks through giving Windows PowerShell a git-style prompt with oh-my-posh, plus installing and customizing Windows Terminal (the newer Microsoft terminal) — themes, fonts, background image. Heads up — this article was written in 2019; oh-my-posh has since moved to v3+ which uses different config commands.
